findings.message: New data-layer write/delete path `delete_items_by_prefix` has no span or metric instrumentation. The delete is invisible to observability — no latency span, no row-count metric, and no error logging with the correlation id, so failures and slow bulk deletes cannot be traced or alerted on.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the delete in a tracing span (e.g. `with tracer.start_as_current_span("db.delete_items_by_prefix")`) and emit a metric for the operation latency and the number of rows deleted (return value), matching the observability conventions used elsewhere in the data layer.

findings.message: Unbounded query: `select(Item).where(Item.name.startswith(prefix))` has no limit/pagination and no latency or row-count metric. Unlike `list_items`, which clamps to MAX_PAGE_SIZE, this selects (and then deletes) every matching row, so a broad prefix can load and delete an arbitrarily large result set with no observability on the volume or duration.
findings.suggestion: Bound or batch the delete and emit a row-count/latency metric (or use a bulk `delete()` statement with the affected-row count recorded as a metric) so the size and duration of the operation are observable.
